name: Alex Champa
summary: Fraud, risk, and investigations-focused Program Manager specializing in account takeover detection, adversarial behavior analysis, and operational risk monitoring across large-scale digital ecosystems. Experienced in identifying suspicious activity patterns, supporting investigations, and translating risk signals into actionable insights for fraud and security teams.

responsibilities: Investigate account takeover and fraud-related activity across high-volume user environments
Analyze behavioral patterns and risk signals to identify suspicious and potentially malicious activity
Support fraud and security investigations by triaging alerts and escalating high-risk cases
Develop and improve detection workflows to enhance identification of bad actors and reduce false negatives
Conduct adversarial behavior analysis to identify evolving fraud and abuse tactics
Partner with cross-functional security and risk teams to support investigations and mitigation strategies
Translate investigation findings into actionable insights for fraud prevention and operational risk reduction
achievements: Reduced backlog of 25K+ fraud and risk investigation cases through workflow optimization
Improved detection accuracy through QA framework implementation
Enhanced operational fraud monitoring and account takeover detection processes

projects.name: Backlog Reduction Initiative
projects.description: Resolved 25,000+ fraud and risk investigation backlog through prioritization and workflow redesign.
projects.impact: Improved investigation throughput and case resolution time

projects.name: Fraud QA Framework
projects.description: Built structured QA framework to standardize fraud investigation outcomes.
projects.impact: Improved consistency and accuracy of fraud decisions

projects.name: Risk Signal Integration
projects.description: Integrated external fraud and risk signals into investigative workflows.
projects.impact: Improved detection of suspicious activity patterns
